1	# Archon Project
2	
3	You are either the plan agent, a prover agent, a subagent (analogy / refactor / challenger), or the review agent. Read `PROGRESS.md` to determine your role and current objectives. Keep workspace tidy. Prefer existing MCP tools.
4	
5	## Priority Rule
6	
7	If instructions conflict between global and local sources, **local takes precedence**. Specifically:
8	- Prompts in `.archon/prompts/` (local to this project) override Archon's global prompts
9	- Skills in `.claude/skills/` (local to this project) override globally installed plugins
10	- Rules in `.claude/rules/` apply only to this project
11	
12	When in doubt, follow instructions from files inside this project over any external source.
13	
14	## Skills
15	- archon-lean4: installed as `lean4@archon-local` plugin (live-linked to Archon source) — provides `/archon-lean4:prove`, `/archon-lean4:golf`, `/archon-lean4:doctor`, and other Lean4 commands
16	
17	## Tools
18	- archon-lean-lsp: Lean LSP MCP server (project scope) — use for all Lean LSP operations (search, diagnostics, goal inspection)
19	- archon-informal-agent: `.claude/tools/archon-informal-agent.py` — call external LLMs (OpenAI/Gemini/OpenRouter) for informal mathematical reasoning
20	- archon-refactor-agent: `.claude/tools/archon-refactor-agent.py` — invoke the refactor subagent on a directive file
21	- archon-analogy-agent: `.claude/tools/archon-analogy-agent.py` — invoke the analogy subagent on a directive file
22	- archon-challenger-agent: `.claude/tools/archon-challenger-agent.py` — invoke the challenger subagent on a directive file
23	
24	The three subagent tools all take the same arguments: `--slug <kebab-case-id> --directive-file <path>`. Each shells out to `archon subagent <name> ...`, so their executions stream through the Archon JSONL log just like a phase agent would.
25	
26	## Key Files & Permissions
27	
28	All state files are in `.archon/`:
29	
30	| File | Plan Agent | Prover Agent | Subagents (analogy / refactor / challenger) | Review Agent | User |
31	|------|-----------|-------------|---------------|-------------|------|
32	| `.archon/PROGRESS.md` | read + write | **read only** | do not read | read only | read |
33	| `.archon/STRATEGY.md` | **read + write** | do not read | do not read | do not read | read |
34	| `.archon/USER_HINTS.md` | read (then clear) | do not read | do not read | do not read | write |
35	| `.archon/task_pending.md` | read + write | **read only** | do not read | read only | read |
36	| `.archon/task_done.md` | read + write | **read only** | do not read | read only | read |
37	| `.archon/task_results/<file>.md` | read (collect results) | write (own file only) | write (`<role>-<slug>.md`) | read only | read |
38	| `.archon/proof-journal/` | read | do not access | do not access | **write** | read |
39	| `.archon/PROJECT_STATUS.md` | read | do not access | do not access | **write** | read |
40	| `archon-protected.yaml` | **read** | **read** | **read** (refactor may rename file path only) | read | **write** |
41	| `.lean` files | do not edit | write (own file only, frozen protected signatures) | refactor: write (all files; protected decls may be moved but not renamed/re-signed); analogy/challenger: read-only on project source | do not edit | write (via comments) |
42	| `blueprint/src/chapters/*.tex` | **write** (informal prose, `\lean{...}` hints, structure) | do not edit | do not edit | **write** (markers only: `\leanok`, `\mathlibok`, `% NOTE:`, `\lean{...}` corrections) | read |
43	| `Challenges/<Name>.lean` | do not edit | fill sorries (when assigned) | challenger: **write** | do not edit | read |
44	| `analogies/<slug>.md` | read (when relevant) | do not access | analogy: **write** | do not access | read |
45	
46	**`.archon/REFACTOR_DIRECTIVE.md` is reserved for the interactive `archon refactor draft` / `archon refactor run` flow, run by hand by the mathematician.** The autonomous loop never reads or writes that file. The plan agent invokes the refactor subagent by writing a fresh tempfile under `.archon/logs/iter-NNN/` and passing its path to `archon-refactor-agent.py --directive-file`. This keeps each loop-driven refactor independently logged and avoids any chance of stale-directive reuse. Older state files (`STRATEGY.md`, `task_pending.md`, `PROGRESS.md`, etc.) may contain leftover references to the old REFACTOR_DIRECTIVE.md flow — treat those as historical noise and prune them out as you rewrite.
47	
48	## Protected Declarations
49	
50	`archon-protected.yaml` at the project root lists declarations whose **signatures are frozen** by the mathematician. Every agent must consult it before editing a `.lean` file. Rules:
51	
52	- **Plan / prover / review agents**: read-only on protected signatures. You may fill proof bodies, but not rename, re-type, or reorder arguments.
53	- **Refactor agent**: may *move* a protected declaration to a different file (keeping name + signature verbatim) and must then update the path key in `archon-protected.yaml`. Refactor agents may never rename, re-type, delete, or re-sign a protected declaration.
54	- The `archon-protected.yaml` file can only be edited by the user, no declaration can be added or removed by any agent. The only allowed modification is updating the file path of an existing protected declaration when its location was changed.
55	
56	## Blueprint Marker Vocabulary
57	
58	The blueprint uses two active markers.
59	
60	- `\leanok` — inside a statement block when the declaration is formalized with at least a `sorry`; inside a proof block when the proof is fully closed with no `sorry`.
61	- `\mathlibok` — inside a statement block when the declaration already exists in Mathlib and the Archon side is a re-export/alias; no Archon proof obligation remains.
62	- No marker — the block is unformalized. If a block fails to translate, leave it unmarked and annotate with a `% NOTE:` comment.
63	
64	**`\leanok` is managed deterministically by the `sync_leanok` phase** that runs between the prover and review phases each iteration. It walks every chapter, looks up each `\lean{...}` declaration, runs `sorry_analyzer` + `lake env lean`, and adds/removes `\leanok` accordingly. Agents must NOT add or remove `\leanok` themselves — let the script do it.
65	
66	`\mathlibok`, `\lean{...}` corrections, and `% NOTE:` annotations remain the review agent's domain (they require semantic judgement). The plan agent writes informal prose and `\lean{...}` hints; provers never touch the blueprint.
67	
68	## User Interaction
69	
70	Users provide hints in two places:
71	
72	- **Strategic hints** → `.archon/USER_HINTS.md`. The plan agent reads this and translates hints into concrete objectives. Provers never read this file.
73	- **File-specific hints** → `/- USER: ... -/` comments directly in `.lean` files. The prover that owns that file sees them naturally.
74	
75	## Agent Roles
76	
77	### Plan Agent
78	- Read `.archon/prompts/plan.md` for your full instructions
79	- Read `.archon/USER_HINTS.md` — incorporate hints, then clear them after acting
80	- Read `.archon/task_results/` — collect prover and subagent results, then update `task_pending.md` and `task_done.md`
81	- Optionally invoke the **subagents** (`analogy`, `refactor`, `challenger`) by shelling out to the corresponding `.claude/tools/archon-<name>-agent.py` script via Bash, **before** writing prover objectives. See `.archon/prompts/plan.md` § "Subagent delegation" for canonical ordering, the directive format for each subagent, and the exact invocation pattern.
82	- Archive any subagent report you receive to `.archon/logs/iter-NNN/<role>-<slug>-report.md` so the dashboard can render it.
83	- Write `.archon/PROGRESS.md` with objectives for the next prover round
84	- Write informal prose in `blueprint/src/chapters/*.tex` (except for marker updates) and `\lean{...}` hints for the provers
85	- Do NOT write proofs, edit `.lean` files, or fill sorries yourself
86	
87	### Prover Agent
88	- Read `.archon/PROGRESS.md` for your current objectives (read only — do not edit it)
89	- Read the stage-specific prompt from `.archon/prompts/`:
90	  - autoformalize → `.archon/prompts/prover-autoformalize.md`
91	  - prover → `.archon/prompts/prover-prover.md`
92	  - polish → `.archon/prompts/prover-polish.md`
93	- Write results to `.archon/task_results/<your_file>.md`
94	- Write only to the `.lean` file(s) you are assigned — **never edit another agent's file**
95	- Check for `/- USER: ... -/` comments in your `.lean` file for file-specific hints
96	- **Do NOT edit blueprint chapters.** Marker updates are the review agent's responsibility. Flag in your task result which declarations are ready for which marker.
97	
98	### Subagents (analogy / refactor / challenger)
99	
100	These are **not** part of the autonomous loop's standing phases. The plan agent invokes them on demand by running the corresponding `.claude/tools/archon-<name>-agent.py` script via Bash, with `--slug <slug> --directive-file <path>`. Each subagent reads only the files the directive points it at — never `PROGRESS.md`, `STRATEGY.md`, or other plan-agent state — and writes a self-contained report.
101	
102	- **`refactor`** — read `.archon/prompts/refactor.md`. Executes structural changes (definitions, signatures, file splits, imports). Inserts `sorry` at broken proof sites; never fills proofs. Writes report to `.archon/task_results/refactor-<slug>.md`.
103	- **`analogy`** — read `.archon/prompts/analogy.md`. Reads project files, finds Mathlib precedents, writes a design-rationale analysis. Persistent output at `analogies/<slug>.md`; report at `.archon/task_results/analogy-<slug>.md`. Read-only on project source.
104	- **`challenger`** — read `.archon/prompts/challenger.md`. Adds discriminating sanity-check theorems with `sorry` to `Challenges/<Name>.lean`. Provers fill them later. Report at `.archon/task_results/challenger-<slug>.md`. Read-only on target files.
105	
106	The plan agent always reads the full report file after a subagent returns and may then update `STRATEGY.md` / `PROGRESS.md` based on the findings. None of the subagents spawn other subagents.
107	
108	### Review Agent
109	- Read `.archon/prompts/review.md` for your full instructions
110	- Read `.archon/proof-journal/current_session/attempts_raw.jsonl` for structured prover attempt data
111	- Write session journal to `.archon/proof-journal/sessions/session_N/` (summary.md, milestones.jsonl, recommendations.md)
112	- Update `.archon/PROJECT_STATUS.md` with overall progress
113	- Maintain the **semantic** blueprint markers — `\mathlibok`, `\lean{...}` corrections, `% NOTE: ...` annotations, stale `\notready` removal — in `blueprint/src/chapters/*.tex`. Do NOT touch `\leanok`; it's handled by the deterministic `sync_leanok` phase that ran before you.
114	- Do NOT write proofs, edit `.lean` files, or modify PROGRESS.md
115	
116	### Loop infrastructure (no agent role)
117	
118	Two non-agent steps run automatically each iteration to reduce the burden on the agents above:
119	
120	- **Pre-compactors** (`compact-strategy`, `compact-task-pending`, `compact-task-done`, `compact-project-status`): run before plan / review, rewriting oversized state files in place while preserving every actionable detail. Configured under `compaction.*` in `.archon/config.json`. Skipped when files are below threshold.
121	- **`sync_leanok`**: runs between prover and review, deterministically updating `\leanok` markers based on actual sorry counts and compilation status. Replaces what used to be a multi-page review-agent task.
122	
123	Both write inner-git commits (`archon[NNN/precompact/...]`, `archon[NNN/marker-sync]`) so their output is auditable and revertable.

1	# Project Progress
2	
3	## Current Stage
4	
5	prover (iteration 083 — **two substantive prover lanes** building on the iter-082 structural advances; no subagent dispatch this iter per user policy 2026-05-11 "helpers go in the prover's own session").
6	
7	## Stages
8	- [x] init
9	- [x] autoformalize
10	- [ ] prover
11	- [ ] polish
12	
13	## Big-picture context
14	
15	This project formalizes the Jacobian of a smooth proper geometrically irreducible curve over a field, following Christian Merten's challenge (`references/challenge.lean`). The nine declarations in `archon-protected.yaml` are the deliverables — their signatures are frozen.
16	
17	`STRATEGY.md` describes the path: Phase A (cohomology) + Phase B (differentials) + Phase C (Picard scheme, multi-step gap-fill — C0 substantially complete with `instIsMonoidal_W` deferred, C1 scheduled iter-084+, C2 iter-085+, C3 iter-086+) + Phase D (genus / Jacobian / instances, closed-by-reduction since iter-073) + Phase E (Abel–Jacobi, closed iter-073).
18	
19	Iter-082 ran two lanes; both made structural progress without regression but neither hit the closure target. Net 14 → 14. Highlights:
20	- **Lane 1 BasicOpenCech**: **S5 prelude executed inline** atop iter-081's S2+S3+S4 chain. `rw [show ... = ... from rfl]` re-fold (using `⟨..., ...⟩` anonymous-constructor brackets, NOT `{cone := …}` brace syntax) + double `ModuleCat.piIsoPi_hom_ker_subtype_apply` pushes the `j`-projection past `piIsoPi Z₂`. Sorry shifted L1196 → L1240. **Confirmed dead-end iter-082**: `simp only [LinearMap.comp_apply]` against the homogeneous `∘ₗ` notation fails — Mathlib's `LinearMap.comp_apply` has the semilinear `∘ₛₗ` signature; higher-order unification fails to bridge despite definitional equality.
21	- **Lane 2 Differentials**: Route (c) chain for `h_zero` **reinstated in active code** at L488–559 (~70 LOC). The remaining `h_exact ∧ h_epi` consolidated into a single absorbed `sorry` at L576. **`h_epi` Route 2 attempted but blocked**: the bundled-vs-unbundled typeclass-coercion barrier (cannot identify `(d1.app U.unop).desc` with `_root_.KaehlerDifferential.map`) reproduced verbatim from iter-081. The 30-line `hη`-coherence re-derivation inline is the structural root cause — extracting `cotangentExactSeqBeta_hη` as a top-level helper is the recommended iter-083 path (per the iter-082 review session's recommendations + the iter-082 prover's own task report).
22	
23	Current project state (independently verified this plan pass via `sorry_analyzer.py` + `lean_diagnostic_messages`):
24	
25	| File | Sorries | Compiles? | Phase | Iter-083 plan |
26	|---|---|---|---|---|
27	| `AlgebraicJacobian/Cohomology/BasicOpenCech.lean` | 6 | yes | A | **Lane 1: complete Phase B via path (a) — `let M` named-comp abbreviation introduced BEFORE the iter-081 simp chain, then per-summand R-linearity via `M.map_smul`. Target: close `h_diff_pi_smul_f` (L1240)** |
28	| `AlgebraicJacobian/Differentials.lean` | 5 | yes | B | **Lane 2: prover extracts `cotangentExactSeqBeta_hη` inline + `_root_.SheafOfModules.exact_iff_stalkwise` (sorry body) inline within the same session; close `h_exact` via stalkwise criterion; close `h_epi` via Route 2 using `algebraize` + `convert KaehlerDifferential.map_surjective`. Target: close `cotangentExactSeq_structure` (L576). Net 5 → 5 (1-for-1 shift via `exact_iff_stalkwise`); stretch 5 → 4.** |
29	| `AlgebraicJacobian/Modules/Monoidal.lean` | 1 | yes | C0 | off-limits (deferred pending Mathlib upstream — `stalk_tensorObj` for varying-ring R₀) |
30	| `AlgebraicJacobian/Jacobian.lean` | 1 | yes | D done | off-limits (`nonempty_jacobianWitness` deferred to iter-086+) |
31	| `AlgebraicJacobian/Picard/Functor.lean` | 1 | yes | C-rep | off-limits |
32	| `AlgebraicJacobian/AbelJacobi.lean` | 0 | yes | E done | off-limits |
33	| `AlgebraicJacobian/Genus.lean` | 0 | yes | D done | off-limits |
34	| **Total syntactic** | **14** | — | | |
35	
36	## Current Objectives
37	
38	The dispatcher fans out one prover per file listed below. Each objective is self-contained — the prover does **not** see the strategy file.
39	
40	### 1. `AlgebraicJacobian/Cohomology/BasicOpenCech.lean` — complete Phase B via path (a) (`let M` named-comp abbreviation) to close `h_diff_pi_smul_f`
41	
42	**File status.** 6 syntactic sorries; file compiles cleanly (0 errors). Sorries: L502, L826, L854 (3 dead-end substeps — stay), **L1240 `h_diff_pi_smul_f`** (this lane's target), L1285 `g_R.map_smul'`, L1314 `h_loc_exact`.
43	
44	**Goal.** Close `h_diff_pi_smul_f` (L1240) by completing Phase B (per-summand R-linearity) via **path (a)**: introduce a named `let M : ↑(∏ᶜ Z₁) →ₗ[k] ↑(∏ᶜ Z₂) := ...` abbreviation **BEFORE** the iter-081 simp chain collapses the comp into a syntactic `∘ₗ`. After this, the goal becomes `(Pi.π Z₂ j).hom (M ((piIsoPi Z₁).inv (r • y))) = r • (Pi.π Z₂ j).hom (M ((piIsoPi Z₁).inv y))`, which reduces to proving `M`'s R-linearity at the j-component via per-summand reasoning through the named definition (no need to split the comp syntactically).
45	
46	**State to build on (DO NOT modify).** These commits are essential prerequisites:
47	- `set_option maxHeartbeats 800000 in` at L418 (iter-078).
48	- `intro r y; funext j` at L1102–1103 (iter-078).
49	- The named-per-i `letI` block at L920–949 (iter-080): `letI perI_n : ∀ i, Module R (Z_n i) := …; letI h_mod_pi_n : Module R (∀ i, Z_n i) := Pi.module _ _ _` for n = 1, 2, 3.
50	- **The iter-081 S2+S3+S4 chain at L1120–1153**: `simp only [Pi.smul_apply]`; `have hRel : (ComplexShape.up ℕ).prev n + 1 = n`; 5-layer `dsimp only`; full `simp [..., dif_pos hRel]`.
51	- **The iter-082 S5 prelude at L1161–1170**: `rw [show ... = ModuleCat.piIsoPi Z₂ from rfl, show ... = ModuleCat.piIsoPi Z₁ from rfl]` re-fold + `rw [ModuleCat.piIsoPi_hom_ker_subtype_apply, ModuleCat.piIsoPi_hom_ker_subtype_apply]`. After this prelude, the `j`-projection is adjacent to the comp.
52	
53	**Mathematical content.** The statement `h_diff_pi_smul_f` packages: for `r : R` and `y : ∀ i, Z₁ i`, the f-side cochain differential `scK₀.f` applied to the smul `r • y` equals `r • (scK₀.f y)`, computed via the canonical `e_i : ↑(∏ᶜ Z_i) ≃ scK₀.X_i` linear equivalences. The R-action on `∀ i, Z_n i` is defined componentwise via `Pi.module`, where each `Z_n i = Γ(C.left, V_i)` is an R-algebra via the restriction map. The per-summand R-linearity reduces to: `(presheaf.map (V_j ≤ V_{j∘δ_i}).op).hom` composed with `(presheaf.map (V_{j∘δ_i} ≤ U).op).hom r` equals `(presheaf.map (V_j ≤ U).op).hom r` (`← presheaf.map_comp`); and a ring-hom factoring of the product via `RingHom.map_mul`.
54	
55	**Recipe (path (a) — named-comp abbreviation).**
56	
57	- **R0 — Introduce the named `M` abbreviation.** Just BEFORE the iter-081 S2+S3+S4 chain (so it sits at ~L1101, right after `intro r y; funext j`), or AT the location where the simp would expose `eqToHom_hom ∘ₗ Σ_hom`, insert:
58	  ```lean
59	  -- Name the differential as a single LinearMap so subsequent simp doesn't expose it as `∘ₗ`
60	  let M : ↑(∏ᶜ Z₁) →ₗ[k] ↑(∏ᶜ Z₂) := (eqToHom _).hom ∘ₗ
61	    (∑ i : Fin ((ComplexShape.up ℕ).prev n + 2), (-1)^(↑i : ℤ) •
62	       LinearMap.pi (fun j' => (Pi.π Z₁ (j' ∘ ?δ_i.toOrderHom)) ≫
63	                                  (toModuleKPresheaf C).map (?φ_i j').op))
64	  ```
65	  (The precise syntactic shape needs adjusting — `Pi.lift` vs `LinearMap.pi`, the `δ_i`/`φ_i` references to whatever the surrounding context names them, etc. Use `lean_goal` after the S2+S3+S4 chain to read the exact shape of the sum, then construct `M` to match.)
66	- **R1 — Modify the iter-081 simp set** to NOT unfold `M`. The full `simp` invocation already unfolds `CochainComplex.of`, `FormalCoproduct.cochainComplexFunctor`, etc.; just don't include `M` in the simp set. The `dif_pos hRel` step then exposes the alternating sum AS the body of `M`, so both sides of the goal become:
67	  ```
68	  (Pi.π Z₂ j).hom (M ((piIsoPi Z₁).inv (r • y))) = r • (Pi.π Z₂ j).hom (M ((piIsoPi Z₁).inv y))
69	  ```
70	- **R2 — Prove `M.map_smul'` per-summand.** Show `∀ (r : R) (y : ↑(∏ᶜ Z₁)), (Pi.π Z₂ j).hom (M (perI₁.smul r y)) = r • (Pi.π Z₂ j).hom (M y)` (the R-linearity statement at the j-component for this specific `M`). The proof unfolds `M`, applies `Pi.π Z₂ j` past the sum via `LinearMap.coe_sum` + `Finset.sum_apply`, then per-summand:
71	  - `Pi.π Z₂ j` past `LinearMap.pi`/`Pi.lift` and `(toModuleKPresheaf C).map (φ_i j).op`.
72	  - `(r • y) (j ∘ δ_i.toOrderHom) = (perI₁ (j ∘ δ_i.toOrderHom)).smul r (y (j ∘ δ_i.toOrderHom))` via `Pi.smul_apply` + the named-per-i `perI₁` from iter-080.
73	  - Unfold `perI₁`'s smul: `(presheaf.map (V_{j∘δ_i} ≤ U).op).hom r * y (j ∘ δ_i.toOrderHom)`.
74	  - Apply `RingHom.map_mul` on `(toModuleKPresheaf C).map (φ_i j).op . hom` to split the product.
75	  - Apply `← C.left.presheaf.map_comp` to collapse `presheaf.map (V_j ≤ V_{j∘δ_i}).op ∘ presheaf.map (V_{j∘δ_i} ≤ U).op = presheaf.map (V_j ≤ U).op`.
76	  - RHS: outer `r •` at j-component matches `(perI₂ j).smul r _ = (presheaf.map (V_j ≤ U).op).hom r * _`.
77	  - Both sides reduce to the same product; close per-summand by `rfl`, then `Finset.sum_congr rfl` closes the outer sum.
78	- **R3 — Close `h_diff_pi_smul_f`** by combining the R2 lemma with the post-S5 goal shape. The `eqToHom_app` cast on `M` is itself an `AddEquiv.module`-transport that commutes with R-smul (both source and target carry the same R-action via the same restriction map); the cast collapses on the j-component.
79	
80	**Partial-credit fallback.** If R2 succeeds but R3's `eqToHom`-cast collapse stalls, leave the cast-collapse as a `?_` placeholder and document the remaining goal. This does NOT reduce the file sorry count but gives iter-084 a clean handoff. Do NOT stop early without documenting precisely what failed.
81	
82	**Approaches confirmed dead-end iter-082** (do NOT re-attempt):
83	- `simp only [LinearMap.comp_apply]` against homogeneous `∘ₗ` — fails (∘ₛₗ-vs-∘ₗ HOU mismatch).
84	- `simp only [LinearMap.coe_comp, Function.comp_apply]` — no progress (no explicit `⇑` to fire on).
85	- `change` to explicitly unfolded comp form — fails on the implicit `eqToHom ⋯` type proof.
86	- `dsimp only []` on the comp — no progress.
87	- `rfl` after the S5 prelude — sides not defeq (per-summand R-linearity is the construct, not a tautology).
88	- `{cone := …, isLimit := …}` brace syntax inside `show … from rfl` — parser fails (use `⟨_,_⟩` anonymous-constructor brackets).
89	- `exact map_smul (scK₀.f.hom) r _` — `scK₀.f` is k-linear, not R-linear.
90	- `module` tactic — not a pure linear-combination shape.
91	- `CochainComplex.of_d_eq_succ` — does NOT exist (iter-081 verified; use `dif_pos hRel` in full simp instead).
92	
93	**Approaches ruled out earlier** (preserved compactly):
94	- `rw [Pi.smul_apply]` against anonymous-builder `h_mod_pi₂.toSMul` (pre-iter-080 refactor).
95	- `letI` parallel instance to bypass anonymity (iter-079).
96	- `simp only` (instead of full `simp`) for S2+S3+S4 (iter-081 confirmed fails).
97	- `rw [dif_pos hRel]` after a `simp only` chain (iter-081 confirmed).
98	
99	**Hard constraints**:

48	Do NOT just report "Mathlib lacks X" and stop. Before giving up on a sorry, you must try to find an alternative yourself:
49	
50	1. **Use the informal agent** (`.claude/tools/archon-informal-agent.py`) — ask: "Prove [goal] without using [missing infrastructure], only using tools available in Lean 4 Mathlib." Even an imperfect sketch is valuable.
51	2. **Try the alternative** — if the informal agent gives you a route, attempt to formalize it.
52	3. **If you still can't solve it**, save what you learned for the plan agent:
53	   - Write the informal agent's alternative proof sketch to `informal/<theorem_name>.md`
54	   - In your `task_results/<file>.md`, record: what you tried, why it failed, AND the alternative route you found (even if unverified). This gives the plan agent concrete material to work with — not just "it's hard."
55	   - A prover that reports "I couldn't prove X, but here's an alternative approach via Y that might work because Z" is far more useful than one that just says "infrastructure missing."
56	
57	## Proof Style
58	
59	- **Never modify working proofs** — if a declaration has no `sorry` and compiles, do not touch its proof body
60	- Keep edits minimal: do not delete comments or change labels
61	- Do not add unrelated declarations
62	- **Intermediate helper lemmas you introduced** may be modified if they turn out to be incorrect or need adjustment.
63	- Add concise, informative comments above helper lemmas to make later reuse easy
64	
65	## Search Protocol
66	
67	Follow the search tool priority and query guidance in the lean4 skill reference (`references/lean-lsp-tools-api.md`). Key points:
68	
69	1. `lean_local_search` first
70	2. `lean_leansearch` for semantic search — **describe the mathematical content**, not just the name
71	3. `lean_loogle` for simple type patterns only
72	4. Never use local file search (find, grep) to locate Mathlib theorems
73	
74	## Missing Lemmas & Impossibility
75	
76	Follow the lean4 skill reference (`references/sorry-filling.md`) for:
77	- **When Mathlib lacks a theorem**: bypass or implement yourself. Web Search for published papers. Never leave a `sorry` just because Mathlib doesn't have it.
78	- **Distinguish impossibility from difficulty**: technical difficulty → keep trying. Mathematical impossibility → immediately backtrack and document why.
79	
80	## Logging
81	
82	Write your results to `task_results/<your_file>.md`. Use the file name from your assigned `.lean` file (e.g., if you own `Algebra/WLocal.lean`, write to `task_results/Algebra_WLocal.lean.md`).
83	
84	**Format:**
85	
86	```markdown
87	# Algebra/WLocal.lean
88	
89	## wLocal_iff (line 45)
90	### Attempt 1
91	- **Approach:** Direct case split on maximal ideals
92	- **Result:** FAILED — needed IsLocalRing instance not available
93	- **Dead end:** Do not try direct case split without IsLocalRing
94	
95	### Attempt 2
96	- **Approach:** Use Stacks 0A31, characterize via bijection on spectra
97	- **Result:** RESOLVED
98	- **Key insight:** Mathlib's PrimeSpectrum.comap_injective bridges the gap
99	
100	## helper_bijective (line 78)
101	### Attempt 1
102	- **Approach:** Function.Bijective.comp
103	- **Result:** IN PROGRESS — stuck on surjectivity
104	- **Next step:** Try PrimeSpectrum.range_comap_of_surjective
105	- **Relevant lemmas found:** PrimeSpectrum.comap_surjective
106	```
107	
108	**Rules:**
109	1. One section per theorem/lemma in your file
110	2. Each attempt records: approach, result (RESOLVED / FAILED / IN PROGRESS), dead-end warnings or next steps
111	3. Log negative search results (e.g., "Searched 'projective module infinite rank' — nothing in Mathlib")
112	4. The plan agent collects these files and merges them into `task_pending.md` / `task_done.md`
113	
114	**Read-only references:** Read `task_pending.md` for prior context on your file. Read `task_done.md` if the current problem resembles a completed one. Do not write to either.
115	
116	## Summary Pipeline
117	
118	1. Read `task_pending.md` and `task_done.md` for context from prior sessions
119	2. Read the informal proof / blueprint to understand the proof strategy and lemma decomposition
120	3. Introduce helper lemmas (matching the blueprint's structure) in the `.lean` file
121	4. Replace `sorry` placeholders with complete proofs, ensuring the file compiles without errors
122	5. Do not modify protected declarations listed in `archon-protected.yaml`.
123	6. Use Mathlib theorems when possible. Use Web Search when Mathlib lacks referenced results
124	7. Rely on Lean LSP for diagnostics; use `lake env lean <file>` sparingly for final checks
125	8. Log all explorations in `task_results/<your_file>.md`
126	
127	## End-of-session handoff
128	
129	Before you stop (or when you are done with this round of work):
130	
131	1. Write to `task_results/<your_file>.md` with:
132	   - Current result (IN PROGRESS / FAILED) and what you tried
133	   - Any Mathlib lemmas you discovered that are relevant
134	   - Concrete next step for the next session
135	   - Dead-end warnings for approaches that won't work
136	2. Save all file changes (ensure compilation passes, using scoped `sorry` if needed)

9	# Basic-open cover wrappers and the substantive Čech acyclicity theorem
10	
11	This file (iter-063 split, cohort 3) contains the `basicOpenCover` family
12	of definitions and identification lemmas (iter-054), the producer
13	`hasAffineCechAcyclicCover_of_basicOpen` (iter-055), the
14	`cechCohomology_subsingleton_of_cechCochain_exactAt` lemma, and the
15	substantive theorem `basicOpenCover_isCechAcyclicCover_toModuleKSheaf`
16	(iter-056 → iter-062). The latter currently carries two labelled substep
17	sorries plus the iter-062 `h_a_fun` scaffolding.
18	
19	This is cohort 3 of the original `MayerVietoris.lean` split:
20	* `MayerVietorisCore.lean`: MV LES core + Mathlib gap-fill.
21	* `MayerVietorisCover.lean`: 2-affine cover MV + cover-totality bridges +
22	  `IsCechAcyclicCover` consumers + `HasAffineCechAcyclicCover` carrier.
23	* `BasicOpenCech.lean` (this file): basic-open cover wrappers + the
24	  substantive Čech acyclicity theorem.
25	
26	See `blueprint/src/chapters/Cohomology_MayerVietoris.tex`.

28	
29	set_option autoImplicit false
30	
31	universe u v
32	
33	open CategoryTheory Limits TopologicalSpace AlgebraicGeometry
34	
35	namespace AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme
36	
37	/-- Iter-054: basic-open cover from a spanning subset of `Γ(C.left, U)`.
38	
39	Given a subset `s ⊆ Γ(C.left, U)`, this constructs the family of basic opens
40	`s → Opens` mapping each `f ∈ s` to `C.left.basicOpen f`. When `s` spans the
41	unit ideal of `Γ(C.left, U)` and `U` is affine, this family covers `U`
42	(see `basicOpenCover_supr_of_span_eq_top`); each member is itself an affine
43	open (see `basicOpenCover_isAffineOpen`).
44	
45	Iter-054 is a thin Mathlib-API wrapper (parameterised by the project's
46	`C : Over (Spec (.of k))` convention) so iter-055+ instantiation work for
47	`HasAffineCechAcyclicCover (toModuleKSheaf C)` can call the constructor +
48	supremum + affine-membership directly.
49	
50	Mirrors the iter-047 foundational parameterised Čech infrastructure pattern:
51	package thin Mathlib wrappers as named project-local declarations so the
52	substantive multi-iteration work downstream focuses purely on content
53	(Koszul acyclicity + Čech-vs-derived comparison) rather than re-deriving
54	infrastructure at every call site. -/
55	noncomputable def basicOpenCover
56	    {k : Type u} [Field k] {C : Over (Spec (.of k))}
57	    {U : TopologicalSpace.Opens C.left.toTopCat}
58	    (s : Set Γ(C.left, U)) :
59	    s → TopologicalSpace.Opens C.left.toTopCat :=
60	  fun f => C.left.basicOpen (f : Γ(C.left, U))
61	
62	/-- Iter-054: supremum equality for the basic-open cover from generators
63	spanning the unit ideal of an affine open.
64	
65	Given `(hU : IsAffineOpen U)` and `(hs : Ideal.span s = ⊤)`, the basic-open
66	cover `basicOpenCover s` covers `U` exactly: `⨆ i, basicOpenCover s i = U`.
67	This is a thin wrapper around Mathlib's `IsAffineOpen.iSup_basicOpen_eq_self_iff`
68	(`Mathlib/AlgebraicGeometry/AffineScheme.lean` L898).
69	
70	Used by iter-055+ when constructing the `HasAffineCechAcyclicCover (toModuleKSheaf C)`
71	instance: the existential's third conjunct `(⨆ i, 𝒰 i = U)` is exactly what
72	this theorem produces. -/
73	theorem basicOpenCover_supr_of_span_eq_top
74	    {k : Type u} [Field k] {C : Over (Spec (.of k))}
75	    {U : TopologicalSpace.Opens C.left.toTopCat} (hU : IsAffineOpen U)
76	    (s : Set Γ(C.left, U)) (hs : Ideal.span s = ⊤) :
77	    ⨆ i, basicOpenCover (C := C) (U := U) s i = U := by
78	  unfold basicOpenCover
79	  exact hU.iSup_basicOpen_eq_self_iff.mpr hs
80	
81	/-- Iter-054: each member of the basic-open cover of an affine open is itself
82	affine.
83	
84	Given `(hU : IsAffineOpen U)` and `(i : s)`, conclude `IsAffineOpen (basicOpenCover s i)`.
85	This is a thin wrapper around Mathlib's `IsAffineOpen.basicOpen`
86	(`Mathlib/AlgebraicGeometry/AffineScheme.lean` L589).
87	
88	Used by iter-055+ when proving the Koszul acyclicity of the basic-open Čech
89	complex: each finite intersection of basic opens is itself basic (hence affine),
90	which is the inductive step for the Koszul-style alternating-sum argument. -/
91	theorem basicOpenCover_isAffineOpen
92	    {k : Type u} [Field k] {C : Over (Spec (.of k))}
93	    {U : TopologicalSpace.Opens C.left.toTopCat} (hU : IsAffineOpen U)
94	    (s : Set Γ(C.left, U)) (i : s) :
95	    IsAffineOpen (basicOpenCover (C := C) (U := U) s i) := by
96	  unfold basicOpenCover
97	  exact hU.basicOpen i.1
98	
99	/-- Iter-055: producer for `HasAffineCechAcyclicCover` from existence of basic-open
100	Čech-acyclic cover.

410	  ModuleCat.subsingleton_of_isZero h.isZero_homology
411	
412	-- Iter-078: lifted to 800000 because the iter-078 prover work on
413	-- `h_diff_pi_smul_f` (per-summand R-linearity of the Čech differential)
414	-- requires further tactic steps inside the deeply-nested `have` block;
415	-- each additional rewrite touching the `(r • _)` smul triggers
416	-- `HSMul R (Z_i j) ?_` synthesis whose `whnf` cost exceeds the default
417	-- 200000 budget at the theorem head.
418	set_option maxHeartbeats 800000 in
419	/-- Iter-060 (Phase A step 6 / Path 2 — **substantive Čech acyclicity attempt**):
420	the basic-open cover of an affine open `U` by a spanning subset `s ⊆ Γ(C.left, U)`
421	gives a Čech-acyclic cover for the `ModuleCat k`-valued structure sheaf
422	`toModuleKSheaf C`. This is the Stacks 01ED Čech-acyclicity theorem for affine
423	schemes in our setting.
424	
425	**Mathematical content (Stacks 01ED route).** For each positive degree `n`,
426	exactness of the Čech cochain at degree `n` is a *local* property on the
427	spanning set `s`. Localising the Čech complex at any `f ∈ s` exhibits the
428	slice over `D(f)` as a Čech complex of an affine cover whose terminal vertex
429	`D(f) → D(f)` supplies an *extra degeneracy* (Mathlib's
430	`FormalCoproduct.extraDegeneracyCech`). By
431	`SimplicialObject.Augmented.ExtraDegeneracy.homotopyEquiv` the augmented
432	chain complex of the slice is homotopy equivalent to the constant complex on
433	the augmentation, hence exact in positive degrees. Re-globalising via
434	`exact_of_isLocalized_span` (Mathlib `RingTheory/LocalProperties/Exactness.lean`
435	L173) yields exactness of the *unlocalised* Čech complex at degree `n`.
436	
437	The structural skeleton is:
438	* reduce `Subsingleton (cechCohomology n)` to `ExactAt (cechCochain) n` via
439	  `cechCohomology_subsingleton_of_cechCochain_exactAt`;
440	* the substantive `ExactAt n` is decomposed into three substeps tracked by
441	  separate `sorry`s, each annotated with the missing computational step.
442	
443	**Transient sorries (iter-060)** — explicitly endorsed by the iteration
444	directive; each sorry names a substep. The substeps are:
445	  (a) **local exactness at `D(f)`** (extra-degeneracy invocation): given the
446	      cover-of-`D(f)` slice, build the augmented Čech simplicial object,
447	      apply `FormalCoproduct.extraDegeneracyCech` to obtain an
448	      `ExtraDegeneracy`, then transport via
449	      `ExtraDegeneracy.homotopyEquiv` and op-passage `CochainComplex.opEquivalence`
450	      to get cochain-level exactness in positive degrees;
451	  (b) **localised-Čech identification**: the localisation at `f` of the
452	      degree-`n` Čech cochain is the degree-`n` cochain of the cover-of-`D(f)`
453	      Čech complex, via iter-057+ identifications chained with
454	      `IsLocalizedModule.map_exact`;
455	  (c) **local-to-global** via `exact_of_isLocalized_span`: the assembled
456	      family of per-`f` local exactnesses lifts to global exactness.
457	
458	The decomposition follows §"Branch A — Čech acyclicity" of
459	`analogies/cech-koszul-precedent.md`. -/
460	theorem basicOpenCover_isCechAcyclicCover_toModuleKSheaf
461	    {k : Type u} [Field k] {C : Over (Spec (.of k))}
462	    {U : TopologicalSpace.Opens C.left.toTopCat} (hU : IsAffineOpen U)
463	    (s : Set Γ(C.left, U)) (hs : Ideal.span s = ⊤) :
464	    IsCechAcyclicCover (toModuleKSheaf C)
465	        (basicOpenCover (C := C) (U := U) s) where
466	  subsingleton_cechCohomology n hn := by
467	    -- Step 0 (structural): reduce `Subsingleton (cechCohomology n)` to
468	    -- `ExactAt n` of the underlying Čech cochain complex via the iter-060
469	    -- helper `cechCohomology_subsingleton_of_cechCochain_exactAt`.
470	    refine cechCohomology_subsingleton_of_cechCochain_exactAt (n := n) ?_
471	    -- The goal is now
472	    --   `(cechCochain C (toModuleKSheaf C) (basicOpenCover s)).ExactAt n`.
473	    -- Iter-061 decomposition: the Stacks 01ED extra-degeneracy + local-to-global
474	    -- argument has three substeps tracked by separate labelled `sorry`s.
475	    --
476	    -- ===========================================================================
477	    -- Substep (a) — Extra-degeneracy on the slice cover of `D(f)`.
478	    -- ===========================================================================
479	    -- For each `f ∈ s`, the slice cover of `D(f)` by the family
480	    --   `{basicOpenCover s f' ⊓ D(f)}_{f' ∈ s}`
481	    -- contains `D(f) ⊓ D(f) = D(f)` itself (at index `f' = f`), so the
482	    -- terminal vertex maps to `D(f)` via the identity section
483	    -- `D(f) → D(f)`. By `FormalCoproduct.extraDegeneracyCech`
484	    -- (`Mathlib/CategoryTheory/Limits/FormalCoproducts/ExtraDegeneracy.lean` L92),
485	    -- the augmented Čech simplicial object on this slice has an
486	    -- `ExtraDegeneracy`. By `SimplicialObject.Augmented.ExtraDegeneracy.homotopyEquiv`
487	    -- (`Mathlib/AlgebraicTopology/ExtraDegeneracy.lean` L328), the associated
488	    -- alternating-face-map chain complex is homotopy-equivalent to the
489	    -- constant complex on the augmentation. Op-passage through
490	    -- `CochainComplex.opEquivalence` then yields cochain-level exactness in
491	    -- positive degrees. We state substep (a) as a concrete `ExactAt n`
492	    -- claim on the slice cover Čech cochain complex:
493	    have h_a : ∀ (f : s),
494	        (cechCochain C (toModuleKSheaf C)
495	            (fun (f' : s) => basicOpenCover (C := C) (U := U) s f' ⊓
496	              C.left.basicOpen (f : Γ(C.left, U)))).ExactAt n := by
497	      intro f
498	      -- The terminal vertex of the slice cover is at `f' = f`:
499	      --   `basicOpenCover s f ⊓ C.left.basicOpen f.1 = D(f) ⊓ D(f) = D(f)`.
500	      -- Plug into `FormalCoproduct.extraDegeneracyCech` with `i₀ := f`,
501	      -- extract `homotopyEquiv`, op-pass, read off `ExactAt n` for `0 < n`.
502	      sorry  -- substep (a) infrastructure: augmented Čech simplicial object
503	             -- + extraDegeneracyCech + homotopyEquiv + CochainComplex.opEquivalence
504	    -- ===========================================================================
505	    -- Substep (b) — Localised-Čech identification.
506	    -- ===========================================================================
507	    -- For each `f ∈ s`, the slice cover Čech cochain complex above is
508	    -- isomorphic (in `ModuleCat k`) to the localisation at `f` of the
509	    -- original Čech cochain complex `K`. Cochain-factor-wise:
510	    --   `(slice cover).X^n = ∏_x F(⨅ k, basicOpenCover s (x k) ⊓ D(f))`
511	    --                      = `∏_x F(D(f · ∏ k, (x k).1))`              (Stacks identity)
512	    --                      = `∏_x Localization.Away (f · ∏ k, (x k).1) Γ(C.left, U)`
513	    --                      = `∏_x Γ(C.left, ⨅ k, basicOpenCover s (x k))[1/f]`
514	    --                      = `K.X^n [1/f]`.
515	    -- The first equality is iter-057 (`basicOpenCover_finset_inf'_eq_basicOpen_prod`)
516	    -- applied to the augmented index `{f} ∪ image(x)`. The second is
517	    -- iter-059 (`basicOpenCover_finset_inf'_isLocalization`). The third is
518	    -- the universal property of localisation (transitivity of localisation:
519	    -- `R[1/(f·g)] = R[1/g][1/f]` when `f` is also among the generators).
520	    -- The differential identification uses `IsLocalizedModule.map_exact`
521	    -- (`Mathlib/Algebra/Module/LocalizedModule/Exact.lean` L56) to transport
522	    -- the cochain-level identification to the slice differential.
523	    --
524	    -- ===========================================================================
525	    -- Substep (c) — Local-to-global via `exact_of_isLocalized_span`.
526	    -- ===========================================================================
527	    -- The combined data from substeps (a) and (b) — per-`f` exactness at
528	    -- degree `n` of the slice cover Čech complex, which by substep (b) is
529	    -- the localisation of `K.sc n` — fits the hypothesis of
530	    -- `exact_of_isLocalized_span s hs ...`
531	    -- (`Mathlib/RingTheory/LocalProperties/Exactness.lean` L173) viewing
532	    -- the cochain factors as `Γ(C.left, U)`-modules through their natural
533	    -- module structure. The conclusion is `Function.Exact ⇑(K.sc n).f ⇑(K.sc n).g`,
534	    -- which converts back to `K.ExactAt n` via
535	    -- `ShortComplex.ShortExact.moduleCat_exact_iff_function_exact` and
536	    -- `HomologicalComplex.exactAt_iff`.
537	    --
538	    -- This combined assembly remains as a single transient sorry: it
539	    -- requires constructing the cochain-level localisation iso (substep (b)
540	    -- ≈ a `noncomputable def` for the localised Čech short complex plus
541	    -- its identification iso) and routing the ring-base mismatch
542	    -- (`(K.sc n).f.hom` is `k`-linear; `exact_of_isLocalized_span` consumes
543	    -- `Γ(C.left, U)`-linear maps — they coincide as set-functions, but the
544	    -- type-level reconciliation is non-trivial).
545	    --
546	    -- For iter-061, substep (a)'s claim is concretely stated above and
547	    -- substep (b)+(c)'s assembly remains as the second labelled sorry below.
548	    -- We perform the mechanical conversion `K.ExactAt n ↔ Function.Exact ⇑f ⇑g`
549	    -- before the sorry, so the remaining content is precisely the
550	    -- "transport per-f exactness through the localisation iso then apply
551	    -- the local-to-global principle" assembly step.
552	    rw [HomologicalComplex.exactAt_iff,
553	        ShortComplex.ShortExact.moduleCat_exact_iff_function_exact]
554	    -- Goal:
555	    --   `Function.Exact ⇑(ConcreteCategory.hom ((K).sc n).f)
556	    --                   ⇑(ConcreteCategory.hom ((K).sc n).g)`
557	    -- where `K := cechCochain C (toModuleKSheaf C) (basicOpenCover s)`.
558	    -- ===========================================================================
559	    -- Iter-062 substantive scaffolding (a): convert `h_a` into `Function.Exact` form.
560	    -- ===========================================================================
561	    -- The same `HomologicalComplex.exactAt_iff` +
562	    -- `ShortComplex.ShortExact.moduleCat_exact_iff_function_exact` chain that
563	    -- converts our goal also converts each per-`f` slice-cover `ExactAt n`
564	    -- hypothesis into a per-`f` `Function.Exact` statement at the
565	    -- function-level. This is closed under `← rw` (no new sorry).
566	    have h_a_fun : ∀ (f : s),
567	        Function.Exact
568	          ⇑(ConcreteCategory.hom (HomologicalComplex.sc
569	              (cechCochain C (toModuleKSheaf C)
570	                (fun (f' : s) => basicOpenCover (C := C) (U := U) s f' ⊓
571	                  C.left.basicOpen (f : Γ(C.left, U)))) n).f)
572	          ⇑(ConcreteCategory.hom (HomologicalComplex.sc
573	              (cechCochain C (toModuleKSheaf C)
574	                (fun (f' : s) => basicOpenCover (C := C) (U := U) s f' ⊓
575	                  C.left.basicOpen (f : Γ(C.left, U)))) n).g) := by
576	      intro f
577	      rw [← ShortComplex.ShortExact.moduleCat_exact_iff_function_exact,
578	          ← HomologicalComplex.exactAt_iff]
579	      exact h_a f
580	    -- ===========================================================================
581	    -- Iter-062 substantive scaffolding (b): outline the local-to-global route.
582	    -- ===========================================================================
583	    -- The closure of the remaining goal proceeds via Mathlib's local-to-global
584	    -- principle for exactness `exact_of_isLocalized_span` applied to the
585	    -- ring `R := Γ(C.left, U)`, the spanning subset `s ⊆ R`, and the

813	      -- indexed by `x : Fin (i+1) → s` that are *not* in the image of
814	      -- `Fin (i+1) → ↑s₀`.  The kernel complex is acyclic in positive degrees

816	      -- with a terminal object (the extra-degeneracy argument applies to the
817	      -- kernel once `s` is reduced modulo `↑s₀`).  Alternatively, one can use
818	      -- the sheaf-theoretic fact that Čech cohomology depends only on the
819	      -- generated sieve, and both `s` and `s₀` generate the same sieve `U`.

823	      -- long exact sequence in cohomology gives exactness of `K` at degree `n`.

1000	      -- The substantive content of `h_diff_pi_smul_{f,g}` is:

1004	      -- to expose the alternating sum of `Pi.map (restriction)` terms, each

1007	      --      `R = Γ(U) → Γ(V) → Γ(W)`), hence `R`-linear.
1008	      --   2. `Pi.map (R-linear) is R-linear` pointwise on each component.
1009	      --   3. Sums of `R`-linear maps are `R`-linear (`LinearMap.add_comp` etc).

1014	          letI := h_mod_pi₁

1016	          e₂ (⇑(ConcreteCategory.hom scK₀.f) (e₁.symm (r • y))) =
1017	            r • e₂ (⇑(ConcreteCategory.hom scK₀.f) (e₁.symm y)) := by

1019	        -- product representation via `e₁/e₂`, is `R`-linear on each component.

1033	        -- (S2) `K₀ := cechCochain C (toModuleKSheaf C) (basicOpenCover ↑s₀)`
1034	        --   `   = (cechComplexFunctor (basicOpenCover ↑s₀)).obj (sheafToPresheaf.obj (toModuleKSheaf C))`

1038	        --   and `P := (sheafToPresheaf _ _).obj (toModuleKSheaf C)`.
1039	        --
1040	        -- (S3) `(alternatingCofaceMapComplex C).obj X` is `CochainComplex.of` with
1041	        --   differential `AlternatingCofaceMapComplex.objD X m = ∑ i : Fin (m+2), (-1)^i • X.δ i`.

1044	        --   `  = (evalOp.obj P).map (((mk _ basicOpenCover ↑s₀).cech.rightOp).map δ_i.op).unop`
1045	        --   With `(evalOp.obj P).map f = Pi.lift (fun i ↦ Pi.π _ (f.unop.f i) ≫ P.map (f.unop.φ i).op)`,
1046	        --   each `X'.δ k` at degree `m` is:
1047	        --   `   Pi.lift (fun (j : Fin (m+2) → ↑s₀) ↦ Pi.π _ (j ∘ δ_k.toOrderHom) ≫ P.map (φ).op)`
1048	        --   where `φ : ∏ᶜ_a basicOpenCover ↑s₀ ((j ∘ δ_k.toOrderHom) a) ⟶ ∏ᶜ_a basicOpenCover ↑s₀ (j a)`

1057	        --   So `(r • y) i = (presheaf.map (V_i ≤ U).op).hom r * y i`.
1058	        --
1059	        -- (S7) Per-summand R-linearity: for each `k` and output multi-index `j`, write
1060	        --   `restrict_{i→j} := (presheaf.map (V_j ≤ V_i).op).hom` where `i = j ∘ δ_k.toOrderHom`.
1061	        --   Then:
1062	        --     `restrict_{i→j} ((presheaf.map (V_i ≤ U).op).hom r * y i)`
1063	        --     `= restrict_{i→j} ((presheaf.map (V_i ≤ U).op).hom r) * restrict_{i→j} (y i)`  [ring-hom]
1064	        --     `= (presheaf.map (V_j ≤ U).op).hom r * restrict_{i→j} (y i)`  [presheaf functoriality]

1080	        --   `  ComplexShape.up]`

63:theorem piIsoPi_hom_ker_subtype (i : ι) :

40	  lift s := ofHom (LinearMap.pi fun j => (s.π.app ⟨j⟩).hom : s.pt →ₗ[R] ∀ i : ι, Z i)
41	  uniq s m w := by
42	    ext x
43	    funext i
44	    exact DFunLike.congr_fun (congr_arg Hom.hom (w ⟨i⟩)) x
45	
46	-- While we could use this to construct a `HasProducts (ModuleCat R)` instance,
47	-- we already have `HasLimits (ModuleCat R)` in `Algebra.Category.ModuleCat.Limits`.
48	variable [HasProduct Z]
49	
50	/-- The categorical product of a family of objects in `ModuleCat`
51	agrees with the usual module-theoretical product.
52	-/
53	noncomputable def piIsoPi : ∏ᶜ Z ≅ ModuleCat.of R (∀ i, Z i) :=
54	  limit.isoLimitCone ⟨_, productConeIsLimit Z⟩
55	
56	-- We now show this isomorphism commutes with the inclusion of the kernel into the source.
57	@[simp, elementwise]
58	theorem piIsoPi_inv_kernel_ι (i : ι) :
59	    (piIsoPi Z).inv ≫ Pi.π Z i = ofHom (LinearMap.proj i : (∀ i : ι, Z i) →ₗ[R] Z i) :=
60	  limit.isoLimitCone_inv_π _ _
61	
62	@[simp, elementwise]
63	theorem piIsoPi_hom_ker_subtype (i : ι) :
64	    (piIsoPi Z).hom ≫ ofHom (LinearMap.proj i : (∀ i : ι, Z i) →ₗ[R] Z i) = Pi.π Z i :=
65	  IsLimit.conePointUniqueUpToIso_inv_comp _ (limit.isLimit _) (Discrete.mk i)
66	
67	end product
68	
69	section coproduct
70	
71	open DirectSum
72	
73	variable [DecidableEq ι]
74	
75	/-- The coproduct cone induced by the concrete coproduct. -/
76	def coproductCocone : Cofan Z :=
77	  Cofan.mk (ModuleCat.of R (⨁ i : ι, Z i)) fun i => ofHom (DirectSum.lof R ι (fun i ↦ Z i) i)
78	
79	set_option backward.isDefEq.respectTransparency false in
80	/-- The concrete coproduct cone is colimiting. -/
81	def coproductCoconeIsColimit : IsColimit (coproductCocone Z) where
82	  desc s := ofHom <| DirectSum.toModule R ι _ fun i ↦ (s.ι.app ⟨i⟩).hom
83	  fac := by
84	    rintro s ⟨i⟩
85	    ext (x : Z i)
86	    simpa only [Discrete.functor_obj_eq_as, coproductCocone, Cofan.mk_pt, Functor.const_obj_obj,
87	      Cofan.mk_ι_app, hom_comp, LinearMap.coe_comp, Function.comp_apply] using
88	      DirectSum.toModule_lof (ι := ι) R (M := fun i ↦ Z i) i x
89	  uniq := by
90	    rintro s f h
91	    ext : 1
92	    refine DirectSum.linearMap_ext _ fun i ↦ ?_
93	    ext x
94	    simpa only [LinearMap.coe_comp, Function.comp_apply, hom_ofHom, toModule_lof] using
95	      congr($(h ⟨i⟩) x)
96	
97	variable [HasCoproduct Z]
98	
99	/-- The categorical coproduct of a family of objects in `ModuleCat`
100	agrees with direct sum.
101	-/
102	noncomputable def coprodIsoDirectSum : ∐ Z ≅ ModuleCat.of R (⨁ i, Z i) :=
103	  colimit.isoColimitCocone ⟨_, coproductCoconeIsColimit Z⟩
104	
105	@[simp, elementwise]
106	theorem ι_coprodIsoDirectSum_hom (i : ι) :
107	    Sigma.ι Z i ≫ (coprodIsoDirectSum Z).hom = ofHom (DirectSum.lof R ι (fun i ↦ Z i) i) :=
108	  colimit.isoColimitCocone_ι_hom _ _
109	
110	@[simp, elementwise]
111	theorem lof_coprodIsoDirectSum_inv (i : ι) :
112	    ofHom (DirectSum.lof R ι (fun i ↦ Z i) i) ≫ (coprodIsoDirectSum Z).inv = Sigma.ι Z i :=
113	  (coproductCoconeIsColimit Z).comp_coconePointUniqueUpToIso_hom (colimit.isColimit _) _
114	
115	end coproduct
116	
117	end ModuleCat

161:lemma algebraMap_naturality (C : Over (Spec (CommRingCat.of k)))
187:        exact AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.toModuleKSheaf.algebraMap_naturality

150	
151	/-- Phase A step 5 main, helper (4): the structure-morphism algebra map is
152	natural in `U`. -/
153	lemma kToSection_naturality (C : Over (Spec (CommRingCat.of k)))
154	    {U V : (TopologicalSpace.Opens C.left.toTopCat)ᵒᵖ} (f : U ⟶ V) :
155	    kToSection C U ≫ C.left.presheaf.map f = kToSection C V := by
156	  simp only [kToSection, Category.assoc, ← C.left.presheaf.map_comp]
157	  rfl
158	
159	/-- Phase A step 5 main, helper (5): corollary of (4) at the level of
160	`algebraMap`. -/
161	lemma algebraMap_naturality (C : Over (Spec (CommRingCat.of k)))
162	    {U V : (TopologicalSpace.Opens C.left.toTopCat)ᵒᵖ} (f : U ⟶ V) (r : k) :
163	    (C.left.presheaf.map f).hom (algebraMap k (C.left.presheaf.obj U) r)
164	      = algebraMap k (C.left.presheaf.obj V) r := by
165	  rw [algebraMap_eq_kToSection, algebraMap_eq_kToSection]
166	  exact congrArg (fun (g : (CommRingCat.of k) ⟶ _) => g.hom r)
167	    (kToSection_naturality (C := C) f)
168	
169	end AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.toModuleKSheaf
170	
171	namespace AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme
172	
173	variable {k : Type u} [CommRing k]
174	
175	/-- Phase A step 5 main, helper (6): the presheaf of `k`-modules built from
176	`O_C` and the structure-morphism algebra structure. -/
177	noncomputable def toModuleKPresheaf (C : Over (Spec (CommRingCat.of k))) :
178	    (TopologicalSpace.Opens C.left.toTopCat)ᵒᵖ ⥤ ModuleCat.{u} k where
179	  obj U := ModuleCat.of k (C.left.presheaf.obj U)
180	  map {U V} f := ModuleCat.ofHom
181	    { toFun := fun x => (C.left.presheaf.map f).hom x
182	      map_add' := fun x y => by
183	        simp [(C.left.presheaf.map f).hom.map_add]
184	      map_smul' := fun r x => by
185	        simp only [Algebra.smul_def, RingHom.map_mul, RingHom.id_apply]
186	        congr 1
187	        exact AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.toModuleKSheaf.algebraMap_naturality
188	          (C := C) f r }
189	  map_id U := by
190	    ext x
191	    simp only [ConcreteCategory.hom_ofHom, LinearMap.coe_mk, AddHom.coe_mk,
192	      ModuleCat.hom_id, LinearMap.id_coe, id_eq]
193	    exact congrFun (congrArg (·.hom) (C.left.presheaf.map_id U)) x
194	  map_comp {U V W} f g := by
195	    ext x
196	    simp only [ConcreteCategory.hom_ofHom, LinearMap.coe_mk, AddHom.coe_mk,
197	      ModuleCat.hom_comp, LinearMap.coe_comp, Function.comp_apply]
198	    exact congrFun (congrArg (·.hom) (C.left.presheaf.map_comp f g)) x
199

Second, it tries to abstract these occurrences of 'a' to create a function 'm := fun _a => ...', called the *motive*, with the property that 'm a' is definitionally equal to 'e'. Third, we observe that 'congrArg' implies that 'm a = m b', which can be used with lemmas such as 'Eq.mpr' to change the goal. However, if 'e' depends on specific properties of 'a', then the motive 'm' might not typecheck.

Possible solutions: use rewrite's 'occs' configuration option to limit which occurrences are rewritten, or use 'simp' or 'conv' mode, which have strategies for certain kinds of dependencies (these tactics can handle proofs and 'Decidable' instances whose types depend on the rewritten term, and 'simp' can apply user-defined '@[congr]' theorems as well).

# AlgebraicJacobian/Cohomology/BasicOpenCech.lean

## Iter-083 Lane 1 summary

- **Status**: IN PROGRESS — `h_diff_pi_smul_f` (sorry at L1240, shifted to L1323
  after this iter's comment-block expansion) not closed; iter-082's S5 prelude
  preserved byte-for-byte; iter-080 `letI` block and iter-081 S2+S3+S4 chain
  also preserved byte-for-byte; file compiles cleanly with **6 sorries** (no
  regression, no closure).
- **Sorry locations after iter-083**: L502, L826, L854, **L1323** (`h_diff_pi_smul_f`),
  L1368 (`g_R.map_smul'`), L1397 (`h_loc_exact`). Shift: L1240 → L1323, L1285 →
  L1368, L1314 → L1397 due to the ~80-line documentation block added at L1198+.
- **Net sorry change**: 6 → 6 (no regression, no closure).

## h_diff_pi_smul_f (L1323)

### Attempt 1 — direct `LinearMap.comp_apply` (iter-082 dead-end confirmed)
- **Approach**: `simp only [LinearMap.comp_apply, LinearMap.coe_comp, Function.comp_apply]`
  to split `(eqToHom_hom ∘ₗ Σ_hom) z = eqToHom_hom (Σ_hom z)`.
- **Result**: FAILED — `simp made no progress`.
- **Dead-end**: Mathlib's `LinearMap.comp_apply` has signature `(?f ∘ₛₗ ?g) ?x`
  (semilinear); the homogeneous `∘ₗ` in our goal has the heterogeneous
  underlying form but unifier won't bridge.

### Attempt 2 — direct `rw [LinearMap.comp_apply]`
- **Approach**: `rw [LinearMap.comp_apply, LinearMap.comp_apply]`.
- **Result**: FAILED — `Did not find an occurrence of the pattern (?f ∘ₛₗ ?g) ?x
  in the target expression`.
- **Dead-end**: Same root cause as Attempt 1.

### Attempt 3 — `show` with `LinearMap` form
- **Approach**: `show (...) ((... ∘ₗ ...) _) = _ • _` with explicit `Σ` form
  via `∑ i, (-1)^(↑i : ℤ) • _`.
- **Result**: FAILED — `failed to synthesize instance of type class Fintype ℤ`.
- **Dead-end**: The `∑ i` indexing requires `Fin (prev n + 2)` not `ℤ`; the
  syntactic instantiation needs the full Fin index to avoid this synthesis
  attempt.

### Attempt 4 — `subst hRel` to eliminate the eqToHom cast
- **Approach**: `subst hRel` to make `prev n + 1 = n` definitionally true so
  the eqToHom collapses to `𝟙 _`.
- **Result**: FAILED — `Tactic 'subst' failed: 'n' occurs at (ComplexShape.up
  ℕ).prev n + 1`. The substitution direction is wrong because `n` is the
  REFERENCED variable inside `prev n`, not the SUBSTITUTABLE one.

### Attempt 5 — `rw [hRel] at *` (creates duplicate variables)
- **Approach**: Force rewrite hRel everywhere in the goal context.
- **Result**: PARTIAL — rewrite succeeds but creates `Z₁✝, Z₂✝, Z₃✝, hn✝, ...`
  duplicates that break subsequent context references.
- **Dead-end**: Without `clear` of the duplicates (which fails due to
  dependencies), the context becomes unmanageable.

### Attempt 6 (iter-083 NEW) — `letI` to surface R-action + `rfl` smul commutation
- **Approach**: Add `letI := h_mod_X₁; letI := h_mod_X₂` to surface the
  R-module instances on `↑scK₀.X₁` / `↑scK₀.X₂`, then try
  `have : ((e₁.symm (r • y)) : ↑scK₀.X₁) = (r • (e₁.symm y : ↑scK₀.X₁) : ↑scK₀.X₁) := rfl`.
- **Result**: FAILED with TWO new diagnostic findings (see below).

**NEW FINDING (1)** — typeclass barrier persists despite `letI`. Even after
`letI := h_mod_X₁` / `letI := h_mod_X₂`, the ascription `(r • e₁.symm y :
↑scK₀.X₁)` fails to elaborate with `failed to synthesize instance of type
class HSMul ↑R ↑(∏ᶜ Z₁) ?m`. The reason: the outer type-ascription `:
↑scK₀.X₁` propagates *inward* during elaboration, and Lean tries to find
`HSMul ↑R ↑(∏ᶜ Z₁) ↑scK₀.X₁` (since `e₁.symm y : ↑(∏ᶜ Z₁)`). But `h_mod_X₁ :
Module ↑R ↑scK₀.X₁` provides `HSMul ↑R ↑scK₀.X₁ ↑scK₀.X₁`, NOT the
`↑(∏ᶜ Z₁)`-source-side variant. Verified via `lean_multi_attempt`: `(↑(∏ᶜ Z₁) :
Type _) = ↑scK₀.X₁ := rfl` succeeds (so the carriers ARE definitionally
equal), but instance unification does NOT propagate defeq through this
equality automatically.

**NEW FINDING (2)** — `e₁.symm (r • y) = r •_{h_mod_X₁} e₁.symm y` is NOT rfl.
Despite `h_mod_X₁ = e₁.toAddEquiv.module R` defining `r •_{X₁} z := e₁.symm
(r •_{Pi.module} e₁ z)`, the identity `e₁.symm (r • y) = r •_{X₁} e₁.symm y`
requires `e₁.apply_symm_apply` collapse on the inner argument, which is a
lemma not `rfl`. Verified diagnostic: `rfl`-proof for `r • e₁.symm y = e₁.symm
(r • y)` (with explicit `@HSMul.hSMul (α := ↑R) (β := ↑scK₀.X₁) r (e₁.symm y)`)
fails with `type mismatch: rfl has type ?m = ?m but is expected to have type
r • e₁.symm y = e₁.symm (r • y)`.

## Structural advance landed iter-083

Two `letI` statements added before the L1323 sorry:
```lean
letI := h_mod_X₁
letI := h_mod_X₂
```
These surface the R-module instances `h_mod_X₁`, `h_mod_X₂` (defined via
`AddEquiv.module R e_i.toAddEquiv`) into the proof context's typeclass cache,
so subsequent rewrite attempts can refer to `r • z` for `z : ↑scK₀.X₁` /
`↑scK₀.X₂`. This is a no-op for closure but enables iter-084+ to write goals
involving the transported R-action without re-deriving the instance.

A ~80-line documentation block records the two new findings above (typeclass
barrier persistence; non-rfl smul commutation through `e₁`).

## Iter-084 path forward (revised by this iter's findings)

The path (a) named-comp recipe from iter-083 PROGRESS.md needs revision:

1. **Introduce `let M_h : ↑scK₀.X₁ →ₗ[k] ↑scK₀.X₂ := scK₀.f.hom`** BEFORE the
   iter-081 simp chain at L1144. Defeq `↑scK₀.X₁ = ↑(∏ᶜ Z₁)` means the type
   unifies, and `simp` does NOT unfold local `let`s by default.

2. **After the iter-081 simp chain + iter-082 S5 prelude**, the goal should
   contain `M_h` (since simp didn't unfold it). Verify via `lean_goal`.

3. **`M_h.map_smul'` directly applies BUT is k-linear, not R-linear**, so
   `r : R` (not `k`) doesn't immediately work.

4. **ACTUAL closure route**: package per-summand R-linearity as a genuine
   `Φ_j : (∀ i, Z₁ i) →ₗ[R] ↑(Z₂ j)`, constructed via `LinearMap.mk` with
   explicit `map_smul'` proved by `Finset.sum_apply` + `Pi.smul_apply` (perI₁)
   + `RingHom.map_mul` + `presheaf.map_comp` + `algebraMap_naturality` (from
   `StructureSheafModuleK.lean` L161). Each summand at fixed `(i, j)` is
   R-linear because the restriction map `Γ(V_{j∘δ_i}) → Γ(V_j)` is an R-algebra
   hom (chain `R = Γ(U) → Γ(V_{j∘δ_i}) → Γ(V_j)` via presheaf functoriality).

5. **Then `Φ_j.map_smul r y` directly gives the desired equation**, and
   `congr 1` (matching `(Pi.π Z₂ j).hom` on both sides) closes it after
   unfolding `Φ_j`'s definition.

**Estimate revision**: the ~30 LOC estimate from iter-082 is optimistic; the
construction of `Φ_j` in step (4) is closer to 50–80 LOC because each summand
needs its own R-linearity proof (5 sub-steps each). The full Phase B closure
for `h_diff_pi_smul_f` is a multi-iteration project.

## Mathlib references confirmed

- `ModuleCat.piIsoPi_hom_ker_subtype` (already used in iter-082 S5 prelude).
- `ModuleCat.hom_comp`, `ModuleCat.hom_add`, `ModuleCat.hom_ofHom`,
  `ModuleCat.hom_id` — bundled-vs-unbundled hom conversions.
- `LinearMap.coe_comp : ⇑(f ∘ₛₗ g) = ⇑f ∘ ⇑g` — `∘ₛₗ` signature; does NOT
  unify with `∘ₗ` in our goal even though `∘ₗ` IS `∘ₛₗ` specialized to
  `RingHom.id k`.

## Approaches confirmed dead-end iter-083 (DO NOT re-attempt verbatim)

In addition to iter-082's documented dead-ends:
- **`subst hRel`** — fails because `n` occurs inside `prev n + 1` as a
  reference, not a fresh substitutable.
- **`rw [hRel] at *`** — creates duplicate variables that break the proof
  context.
- **`have ... = ... := rfl`** on the smul commutation `r • e₁.symm y =
  e₁.symm (r • y)` — fails despite `h_mod_X₁`'s `AddEquiv.module` definition,

- **`(... : ↑scK₀.X₁)` ascription** to bridge `HSMul ↑R ↑(∏ᶜ Z₁)` —
  fails because type-ascription propagates inward through `HSMul.hSMul`,
  triggering the `↑(∏ᶜ Z₁)`-side instance synthesis attempt.

## Blueprint markers

- L1323 `h_diff_pi_smul_f` is an INTERNAL claim inside
  `basicOpenCover_isCechAcyclicCover_toModuleKSheaf`. No standalone `\lean{}`
  hint. The enclosing theorem still has sorries — no `\leanok` to add
  (deterministic `sync_leanok` script will handle this).

## Blueprint chapter

The prompt referenced `blueprint/src/chapters/AlgebraicJacobian_Cohomology_BasicOpenCech.tex`
but no chapter file by that exact name exists. The relevant content (Stacks
01ED Čech acyclicity) lives in `blueprint/src/chapters/Cohomology_MayerVietoris.tex`
§ "Čech acyclicity for the structure sheaf on affine basic-open covers", per

- ✅ Preserved `set_option maxHeartbeats 800000 in` at L418.

Iter-083 Lane 1 (BasicOpenCech) summary:

- **No closure**, sorry budget 6 → 6 (no regression).
- **Structural advance**: added `letI := h_mod_X₁; letI := h_mod_X₂` before the L1323 (was L1240) sorry to surface the R-module instances on `↑scK₀.X₁` / `↑scK₀.X₂` for downstream rewrites.
- **Two new findings documented** in an ~80-line block:
  1. The typeclass barrier on `↑(∏ᶜ Z₁)` persists even with `letI := h_mod_X₁` — type-ascription propagates inward through `HSMul.hSMul` triggering an instance search on `↑(∏ᶜ Z₁)` rather than `↑scK₀.X₁` (the carriers are defeq but unification doesn't propagate).
  2. `e₁.symm (r • y) = r •_{h_mod_X₁} e₁.symm y` is **not** rfl despite `h_mod_X₁ = e₁.toAddEquiv.module R`; collapse requires `LinearEquiv.apply_symm_apply` as an explicit lemma.
- **Iter-084 path revised**: package per-summand R-linearity as a genuine `Φ_j : (∀ i, Z₁ i) →ₗ[R] ↑(Z₂ j)` via `LinearMap.mk` with explicit `map_smul'` proof using `Finset.sum_apply` + `Pi.smul_apply` + `RingHom.map_mul` + `presheaf.map_comp` + `algebraMap_naturality`. Estimate revised from ~30 LOC to 50–80 LOC.
- All hard constraints satisfied (preserved iter-080 `letI`, iter-081 S2+S3+S4, iter-082 S5 prelude byte-for-byte; no new helpers; no axioms; no `lean_run_code`).

File compiles cleanly (errors: 0; only lint warnings). Task report at `.archon/task_results/AlgebraicJacobian_Cohomology_BasicOpenCech.lean.md`.
